violations.text: A spoon was observed stored with the handle in contact with the food item (sprinkles/jimmies). Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Spoon was adjusted for proper storage at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026
The light shield to the ceiling mounted light in the downstairs storage closet was observed to be cracked and in need of replacement. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 25 parts per million (ppm) or less. The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Proper chlorine sanitizing solution was made and tested at 50-100 ppm at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Spray Bottle Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Proper chlorine sanitizing solution was made and tested at 50-100 ppm at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The establishment does not have an allergen statement present for consumer awareness. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The floors behind the prep tables/refrigeration units in the kitchen area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The walls in the front prep area where cones are being made are constructed of painted wood paneling. This material is not easily cleanable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 09/21/2023
